title: Cyrtochilum prasinum
uses: Cyrtochilum prasinum is known to have medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various ailments. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. It is used to treat conditions such as fever, colds, and skin infections. Additionally, Cyrtochilum prasinum is also used to treat digestive issues and as an antiseptic. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum prasinum is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This species falls within the genus Cyrtochilum, which is a part of the subtribe Cymbidiinae. The subtribe Cymbidiinae is a group of orchids that are native to Central and South America. Cyrtochilum prasinum is further classified within the section Cyrtochilum, which includes several species of orchids. The full classification of Cyrtochilum prasinum is: family Orchidaceae, subfamily Orchidoideae, tribe Maxillarieae, subtribe Cymbidiinae, genus Cyrtochilum, and species C. prasinum.
